channel.title: ENG 104 on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in ENG 104 on RaihnForge

item.title: ENG 104 — Academic Writing II: Sustainability & Civic Engagement
item.description:
Course Snapshot

  • Institution. Central Washington University.
  • Full title. Academic Writing II: Reasoning and Research on Sustainability and Civic Engagement.
  • Catalog description. “Develops skills in research-based academic argument through assignments involving evaluation, analysis, and synthesis of multiple sources.” Offered every year (Fall, Winter, Spring).
  • Credits. 5
  • Prerequisites. ENG 101 with a grade of C- or higher.
  • General-education designation. K1, Academic Writing II: Critical Reading and Responding. One of the required Knowledge Areas in the Explore and Connect section of the CWU General Education program.
  • Thematic frame. Sustainability and civic engagement. The course is one of three thematic ENG 104 variants offered alongside ENG 102 (Social Justice) and ENG 103 (Health and Current Issues); all three teach the same core competencies through a different topical lens.

Official Learning Outcomes

Quoted from the CWU course catalog. Upon completion, a student should be able to:

item.title: Sustain Craft: A Mobile-Game Pitch for Sustainability Literacy
item.description:
A note on form. This artifact is a pitch deck, not a prose essay. What follows describes each section of the deck in the order it was presented, preserving the speaker notes and the verbatim character bio for the protagonist.

1. Pitch

Title. Sustain Craft, a mobile game pitch.

Capstone framing. Application of skills for positive change.

Why this medium. The deck opens by tracing the skills the game draws on: Marine Corps leader, graphic designer and illustrator, animator, game designer, project leader. The argument is that games are the natural medium for me to make a civic argument with, because I already have most of the disciplinary skills the medium demands.
